compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Implemented four containment barriers for the remaining 28 CodeQL alerts (27 py/path-injection + 1 py/stack-trace-exposure):
1. _ids.py — resolve_ticket_id fast path now routes through _existing_ticket_dir_name() (safe-segment guard + normpath containment + basename return); added resolve_ticket_dir_name() that raises FileNotFoundError on hostile/unresolvable ids. Covers the ~18 resolver-fed sinks.
2. completion/plan_review/code_review sidecars — dropped the unsafe 'resolve_ticket_id(id) or id' fallback (uniform swap to resolve_ticket_dir_name; existing FileNotFoundError handlers map to empty).
3. editor_server.py — _raw_prompt_text contained via resolve()+is_relative_to(prompts_dir).
4. review_bot/app.py — /rerun 502 no longer leaks the Gerrit exception string.
New tests: tests/unit/test_path_injection_guards.py (20 tests). 974 blast-radius tests + ruff check/format all pass. Committing to Gerrit next.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Pushed to Gerrit as change 789: https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/789 (commit 95e7213b0). Local pre-commit gate (ruff + full-library mypy) passed. Awaiting LLM-Review + Verified votes; will submit once both green, then close.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

GitHub CodeQL flags 28 remaining open code-scanning alerts on `main`: 27 `py/path-injection`
and 1 `py/stack-trace-exposure`. All are genuine (the false positives were already dismissed
under 5b57). They collapse to a small number of root causes:

1. **Unvalidated ticket-id → filesystem path.** `rebar._ids.resolve_ticket_id()` has a fast
   path that returns the caller's raw string verbatim when it happens to name an existing
   directory (`_ids.py:152`), *before* any regex/allowlist check. A traversing/absolute id
   (`../sibling`, `/etc/x`) is handed back as if canonical, then callers join it to the
   tracker dir. This is the source for ~18 sinks (the reducer read path + `reads.py`).
2. **Sidecar `resolve_ticket_id(id) or id` fallback.** The audit readers in
   `completion_sidecar.py` and `plan_review/sidecar.py` fall back to the *raw* id when
   resolution returns `None`, so even a hardened resolver is bypassed — the raw string builds
   a `os.path.join(tracker, id)` → `os.listdir`/`open` (sinks 112/119/156/163, 184/192).
3. **Editor prompt-id traversal.** The workflow editor server's `/prompt?id=` handler builds
   `<repo>/.rebar/prompts/{pid}.md` from an unvalidated query param (`editor_server.py`
   158/159); the packaged branch is allowlist-guarded but the project-override branch is not.
4. **Stack-trace exposure.** The review-bot `/rerun` 502 returns the Gerrit exception string
   in the response body (`review_bot/app.py:266`).

Trust model: rebar is a single-tenant local tool; the realistic vector is the MCP/CLI
confused-deputy (an agent driven by untrusted ticket content passing a crafted id). Severity
is Low–Medium, but the fixes are low-risk (no legitimate ticket id / prompt id contains a
path separator, `..`, or is absolute) and retire the whole alert class.

## What

Four containment barriers, mirroring the taint map:

- **`_ids.py`** — add `_is_safe_segment(name)` and reject non-segment ids at the top of
  `resolve_ticket_id`; rewrite the fast path to normalize + containment-check the candidate
  against `tracker_dir` and return the directory's `basename` (separator-free by
  construction). Sanitizes the 18 downstream sinks that build paths from the return value.
- **`completion_sidecar.py` / `plan_review/sidecar.py`** — drop the `... or ticket_id`
  fallback in the filesystem-path readers; on `None` return the function's existing empty
  result (`None`/`[]`/`0`). The hardened resolver's fast path already subsumes the fallback's
  only legitimate purpose (an id that already names a real dir), so this is behavior-preserving
  for valid input.
- **`editor_server.py`** — contain the prompt-id path in `_raw_prompt_text` (resolve +
  `is_relative_to` the prompts dir) and reject separator-bearing ids at the handler entry.
- **`review_bot/app.py`** — log the Gerrit exception server-side; return a generic 502 body.

Default CodeQL query suite unchanged; this is a `src/rebar` change plus new unit tests.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `resolve_ticket_id` returns `None` for any id that is not a safe single path segment
      (rejects `../x`, absolute paths, `..`/`.`, `/`/`\` separators, and leading-dot names),
      and its fast path returns a separator-free canonical directory name — so no untrusted id
      escapes the tracker directory. Valid full/short/alias/Jira-key/prefix ids still resolve.
- [ ] The audit sidecar readers in `completion_sidecar.py` and `plan_review/sidecar.py` no
      longer fall back to the raw unresolved id; when resolution fails they return their empty
      result instead of building a filesystem path from unvalidated input.
- [ ] The workflow editor `/prompt` read (`_raw_prompt_text`) cannot read a file outside
      `<repo>/.rebar/prompts/`; a traversing prompt id yields no file (returns `None`/404).
- [ ] The review-bot `/rerun` 502 response body contains no exception/stack-trace text; the
      detail is logged server-side.
- [ ] New unit tests assert traversal rejection for the resolver, one sidecar reader, and the
      editor prompt read, and assert the review-bot 502 body carries no exception detail.
      `make lint` and the affected test suites pass.
compiled_state.title: Harden ticket-id/prompt-id path handling + review-bot error body (retire remaining CodeQL path-injection & stack-trace alerts)
